name: Horcajo de Santiago, Corral de Almaguer, Villanueva de Alcardete – circular route
name_original: Horcajo de Santiago, Corral de Almaguer, Villanueva de Alcardete – ruta circular
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Horcajo de Santiago offer access to the diverse landscapes of Cuenca, Castile-La Mancha. The region features rural roads and forest tracks, providing varied terrain for cyclists. This area is characterized by open plains and agricultural land, with gentle undulations suitable for touring. The network of routes avoids heavy traffic, utilizing quieter paths for exploration. Best touring cycling routes around Horcajo De Santiago The most popular touring cycling route is Horcajo de Santiago, Corral de Almaguer, Villanueva de Alcardete – circular route, a 37.5 miles (60.3 km) trail that takes 5 hours 3 minutes to complete. This route is classified as difficult, offering a substantial cycling challenge. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Horcajo de Santiago — circular from Tarancón, a moderate 32.4 miles (52.1 km) path. This route features varied terrain through the local countryside, often completed in about 3 hours 5 minutes. Local touring cyclists also love the Almenara Castle – Horcajo de Santiago loop from Horcajo de Santiago, a 24.0 miles (38.7 km) trail leading through open landscapes and past historical points of interest, often completed in about 3 hours 22 minutes. Touring cycling around Horcajo de Santiago is defined by rural roads, forest tracks, and open agricultural plains. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult routes. The routes in Horcajo de Santiago are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 1 review. More than 10 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Horcajo de Santiago's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Horcajo de Santiago?
A: There are 7 verified touring cycling routes around Horcajo de Santiago. These routes utilize rural roads, forest tracks, and urban bike lanes, ensuring a focus on safety by avoiding heavy traffic.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Horcajo de Santiago?
A: The terrain around Horcajo de Santiago is characterized by open plains and agricultural land, with gentle undulations. Routes primarily follow rural roads and forest tracks, offering varied scenery suitable for touring cyclists.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of touring cycling routes in this area?
A: The touring cycling routes around Horcajo de Santiago cater to various skill levels. There are 6 moderate routes and 1 difficult route, providing options for both intermediate and experienced cyclists.

Q: When is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Horcajo de Santiago?
A: Given the region's climate, spring and autumn are generally the most pleasant seasons for touring cycling in Horcajo de Santiago. The temperatures are milder, and the natural landscapes are vibrant. Summer can be quite warm, while winter might bring colder conditions.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Horcajo de Santiago?
A: Yes, several routes are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022696040/horcajo-de-santiago-corral-de-almaguer-villanueva-de-alcardete-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Horcajo de Santiago, Corral de Almaguer, Villanueva de Alcardete – circular route&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging option, while &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1357768190/horcajo-de-santiago-circular-from-tarancon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Horcajo de Santiago — circular from Tarancón&lt;/a&gt; offers a moderate ride through varied countryside.

Q: Are there family-friendly touring cycling routes in Horcajo de Santiago?
A: While there are no routes specifically graded as 'easy' in the immediate vicinity, the region's use of rural roads and forest tracks generally means less traffic. Moderate routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20134989" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Horcajo de Santiago loop from Horcajo de Santiago&lt;/a&gt;, which is 35 km long, could be suitable for families with older children or those accustomed to longer rides.

Q: Can I bring my dog on touring cycling routes in Horcajo de Santiago?
A: Many rural roads and forest tracks in the Horcajo de Santiago area are generally dog-friendly, especially if your dog is accustomed to cycling alongside or in a trailer. However, always ensure your dog is well-behaved, on a leash where required, and that you carry enough water for both of you. Specific regulations might apply in certain protected natural areas, so it's always good to check locally.

Q: What interesting landmarks or historical sites can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: The routes around Horcajo de Santiago offer glimpses of local history and culture. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24532304" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almenara Castle – Horcajo de Santiago loop from Horcajo de Santiago&lt;/a&gt; passes by historical points of interest. You can also explore highlights such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6360140" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruined Haciendas of La Calderona&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6855415" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Our Lady of the Castle and Remains of the Castle&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What are some longer touring cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1022696040/horcajo-de-santiago-corral-de-almaguer-villanueva-de-alcardete-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Horcajo de Santiago, Corral de Almaguer, Villanueva de Alcardete – circular route&lt;/a&gt; is classified as difficult. It covers 60.3 km and involves a substantial cycling effort, taking approximately 5 hours to complete.

Q: Are there any moderate touring cycling routes that offer varied scenery?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1357768190/horcajo-de-santiago-circular-from-tarancon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Horcajo de Santiago — circular from Tarancón&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 52.1 km route that features varied terrain through the local countryside. It's a great option for those looking for a scenic ride without extreme difficulty.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Horcajo de Santiago?
A: The touring cycling routes in Horcajo de Santiago are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Cyclists often praise the quiet rural roads, forest tracks, and the opportunity to explore the diverse landscapes of Cuenca away from heavy traffic.

Q: Is public transport available to access the cycling routes in Horcajo de Santiago?
A: Horcajo de Santiago is a municipality in Cuenca, Castile-La Mancha. While local bus services connect it to larger towns, direct public transport options specifically designed for cyclists with bikes might be limited. It's advisable to check local bus schedules and policies regarding bicycle transport if you plan to rely on public transport to reach the starting points of routes.

Q: Where can I park my car when starting a touring cycling route in Horcajo de Santiago?
A: As a rural area, parking is generally available in and around the town of Horcajo de Santiago. You can typically find parking in the village center, near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7583393" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plaza de la Villa&lt;/a&gt; or other public areas. Always ensure you park responsibly and do not obstruct local access.
tips: This town surrounded by vine fields has a large wine production cooperative. As a curiosity, on more than one occasion a sandwich several hundred meters long has been prepared during its patron saint festivities, organized by the Atlético de Madrid supporters club.
The hermitage is near the river in a very quiet environment. It is closed but there is a porch where you can take refuge and rest on rainy days.
It is the first horcajeña church from the 14th century. This spectacular mass stands on the hill and it is inevitable to make a stop to contemplate the beauty that it radiates.
